name: W4 Push Morning Flow
description: 12 min shoulder + chest primer. Different from W3 — scorpion stretch is out, we add thread-the-needle for thoracic rotation and a wall angel for shoulder mobility. Your push is getting stronger; open up the range before you load it.

exercises.notes: Big circles, loosen the joint before loading it
30s each side. From all fours, thread one arm under and through. Opens thoracic rotation — where push press range comes from.
Back flat against wall, arms slide up and down like a snow angel. If you can't keep your back flat, that's the tightness you're working on.
Easy and controlled. Full ROM — this is warming, not working.
Hands close, feel the triceps engage. Not to failure — just activation.
Flow between plank and downward dog. Opens chest and shoulders, warms the pushing muscles.
30s each side. Low lunge, twist toward front knee, reach overhead. Opens hip flexors AND thoracic spine — primes push press.
Hands on a doorframe or wall corner, lean forward gently. Feel the pec stretch. 30s.
All fours, walk hands forward, chest toward floor. Deep shoulder and chest stretch to close out.

tips: 12 min — different from W3's warrior flow. Focus on shoulder mobility before the heavy pressing.
Thread-the-needle is new — go slow, breathe into the rotation. Tight thoracic = limited push press lockout.
Wall angels: if your back peels off the wall, you found your sticking point. Work through it gently.
Puppy pose last — warm muscles stretch deeper. Let gravity do the work.
If short on time: skip chest activation block, just do shoulder opener + stretch
